title: California Driver License Gen 3 Real ID
subtitle: Generation 3 design (issued ~2018–present); REAL ID compliant; star-bearing
sources: REAL ID Act of 2005 / 6 CFR Part 37
California DMV Driver License & ID Card Design Specification (Gen 3)

sections.title: Generation & Variant Identification
hints: Is 'CALIFORNIA' printed as the state name header in a distinctive font across the top of the card?
Gen 3 uses a bold condensed sans-serif typeface for the state name in the header band.

hints.source: California DMV Driver License & ID Card Design Specification (Gen 3)
hints.quote: The word CALIFORNIA shall appear in the header zone in the approved state typeface.

hints: Is 'DRIVER LICENSE' printed beneath the state name in a smaller font on the same header band?
Distinguishes DL from ID Card; both are Gen 3 but labeled differently.

hints.source: California DMV Driver License & ID Card Design Specification (Gen 3)
hints.quote: Document type shall be identified as DRIVER LICENSE or IDENTIFICATION CARD in the header zone.

hints: Is the REAL ID star marking present in the upper-right corner of the card?
A gold star with a white outline appears in the top-right header zone on REAL ID-compliant cards.

hints.source: REAL ID Act of 2005 / 6 CFR Part 37
hints.quote: A REAL ID compliant driver's license or identification card shall bear a star in the upper portion of the card.

hints: Does the REAL ID star appear as a gold five-pointed star with a white center on a dark background?
California's Gen 3 implementation places the star inside a dark blue or black badge shape.

hints.source: REAL ID Act of 2005 / 6 CFR Part 37
hints.section: §37.17(n) and DHS REAL ID Marking Guidance 2008
hints.quote: The star marking shall be a gold bear and star or equivalent state symbol indicating REAL ID compliance.

hints: Is the California state grizzly bear graphic visible in the header design area?
The California republic bear or derivative graphic is present in the header zone of Gen 3.

hints.source: California DMV Driver License & ID Card Design Specification (Gen 3)
hints.quote: The California grizzly bear state symbol shall appear in the document header in accordance with the approved design template.

sections.title: Physical Card Layout & Orientation
hints: Does the card measure 85.6 mm wide × 53.98 mm tall (3.370 in × 2.125 in)?
Standard ID-1 credit card size per ISO 7810.

hints.quote: An ID-1 card shall have nominal dimensions of 85.60 mm × 53.98 mm with a thickness of 0.76 mm.

hints: Is the card orientation landscape (horizontal) with the photograph on the left side of the front face?

hints.quote: Cards issued in landscape format shall position the portrait image on the left portion of the card face.

hints: Is the card thickness approximately 0.76 mm (within ISO tolerance of ±0.08 mm)?
Severely under- or over-thickness cards indicate non-standard substrate.

hints.quote: Nominal thickness shall be 0.76 mm with a permitted tolerance of ±0.08 mm.

hints: Are all four card corners rounded with a radius of approximately 3.18 mm?

hints.quote: ID-1 cards shall have rounded corners with a radius of 3.18 mm ± 0.30 mm.

sections.title: Photograph & Portrait Zone
hints: Is the portrait photograph positioned in the left-center zone of the card front, with the head clearly visible against a plain background?

hints.quote: The portrait image zone shall be located in the left portion of the card face and shall display a full-face frontal view of the cardholder.

hints: Is the photograph a color image (not black-and-white or grayscale)?

hints.quote: The portrait image shall be produced in full color.

hints: Is a ghost image (smaller secondary portrait) present to the right of or below the main portrait?
California Gen 3 includes a ghost image as an additional security feature.

hints.source: California DMV Driver License & ID Card Design Specification (Gen 3)
hints.quote: A secondary ghost portrait image shall appear in a reduced size adjacent to the primary portrait zone.

hints: Does the portrait lack obvious digital halos, pixelation artifacts, or color inconsistencies at the edges?
Image substitution often leaves halos or color fringing around the portrait.

hints.quote: Portrait images shall be free of artifacts that indicate post-issuance manipulation.

sections.title: Document Number
hints: Does the California DL number follow the format of one letter (A–Z) followed by exactly seven digits (e.g., A1234567)?
California uses a letter+7-digit format. Deviations indicate a fraudulent number.

hints.source: California DMV Driver License & ID Card Design Specification (Gen 3)
hints.quote: California driver license numbers consist of one alpha character followed by seven numeric digits.

hints: Is the document number laser-engraved or digitally printed (not embossed or inkjet-printed on the surface)?

hints.quote: Personalized data shall be applied using laser engraving or equivalent secure printing technology.

hints: Is the document number also perforated into the card body as a laser perforation visible when held to light?
California Gen 3 uses laser perforation of the DL number as a covert security feature.

hints.source: California DMV Driver License & ID Card Design Specification (Gen 3)
hints.quote: The document number shall be replicated by laser perforation visible under transmitted light.

sections.title: Dates (Issue, Expiry, Date of Birth)
hints: Are all dates (DOB, issue date, expiry date) formatted MM/DD/YYYY on the card face?
California uses the MM/DD/YYYY format for all printed dates.

hints.quote: Dates shall be displayed in the format MM/DD/YYYY unless otherwise specified by the issuing jurisdiction.

hints: Is the expiration date either 5 years or 10 years from the issue date (depending on applicant age)?
California issues 5-year DLs to those under 70; seniors over 70 receive shorter terms. Most standard DLs: issue + 5 years at next birthday.

hints.quote: A driver's license shall expire on the fifth birthday of the licensee following the date of issuance unless issued to a person 70 years of age or older.

hints: Is the expiry date not more than 8 years from the issue date?
No California DL should have a validity period exceeding 8 years.

hints.source: REAL ID Act of 2005 / 6 CFR Part 37
hints.quote: REAL ID-compliant driver's licenses and identification cards shall expire not more than eight years after the date of issuance.

hints: Is the applicant's age on the issue date consistent with the date of birth and issue date printed on the card?

hints.quote: Issue date, expiration date, and date of birth shall be internally consistent.

sections.title: Physical Descriptors
hints: Is the height field printed in feet-and-inches format (e.g., '5-11' or '5′ 11″')?
California prints height as feet-inches, e.g., '5-09'.

hints.quote: Height shall be expressed in feet and inches in jurisdictions using the U.S. customary system.

hints: Is the eye color abbreviated to one of the AAMVA-standard codes: BLK, BLU, BRN, GRY, GRN, HAZ, MAR, PNK, DIC?

hints.quote: Eye color shall be represented by one of the standardized AAMVA abbreviations.

hints: Is the hair color abbreviated to one of the AAMVA-standard codes: BAL, BLK, BLN, BRO, GRY, RED, SDY, WHI, UNK?

hints.quote: Hair color shall be represented by one of the standardized AAMVA abbreviations.

hints: Is the sex/gender field printed as 'M', 'F', or 'X' (California allows nonbinary designation)?
California introduced the X gender marker in 2019.

hints.source: California Vehicle Code §12811.7 and California DMV Design Spec Gen 3 Amendment 2019
hints.quote: A driver's license or identification card issued to a person who has selected a nonbinary gender designation shall bear the designation 'X'.

sections.title: Visual Security Features
hints: Is a laser-engraved signature of the cardholder present on the card front?

hints.quote: A reproduction of the cardholder's signature shall appear in the designated signature zone.

hints: Is an optically variable device (OVD / hologram) present on the card that shifts color when tilted?
California Gen 3 uses a full-card or partial overlay OVD laminate.

hints.source: California DMV Driver License & ID Card Design Specification (Gen 3)
hints.quote: An optically variable device laminate shall be applied to the card face.

hints: Does the OVD/hologram display the California state seal or bear imagery that shifts between two color states when tilted?

hints.source: California DMV Driver License & ID Card Design Specification (Gen 3)
hints.quote: The OVD shall incorporate the California state seal and bear imagery with color-shift properties.

hints: Is the card background a complex guilloche (fine-line) pattern rather than a plain or simple colored background?
Guilloche printing is produced with specialized equipment and is difficult to reproduce by inkjet or laser.

hints.quote: The card background shall incorporate fine-line security printing (guilloche) to resist reprographic reproduction.

hints: Is microprinting visible under magnification (≥5×) somewhere on the card (e.g., along a border or within the portrait zone)?
Microprinting typically includes repeated state name or document type text.

hints.source: California DMV Driver License & ID Card Design Specification (Gen 3)
hints.quote: Microprinting shall be incorporated in at least one location on the card face and shall be legible only under magnification.

hints: Does the printing show no signs of inkjet dot patterns (visible as a grid of dots under magnification)?
Authentic DLs are produced by offset lithography or laser; inkjet dot patterns indicate counterfeit production.

hints.quote: Security printing shall not be reproducible using consumer inkjet or laser printing technologies.

hints: Is a color-shifting or pearlescent ink feature visible in one or more printed elements on the card?

hints.source: California DMV Driver License & ID Card Design Specification (Gen 3)
hints.quote: Color-shifting or optically variable ink shall be applied to at least one printed feature on the card.

sections.title: UV / Ultraviolet Features
hints: Under UV light (365 nm), does the California state seal or a secondary design element fluoresce?

hints.source: California DMV Driver License & ID Card Design Specification (Gen 3)
hints.quote: UV-fluorescent ink features shall be incorporated into the card design and shall be invisible under white light.

hints: Under UV light, does the card substrate itself appear dull (non-fluorescent), with only intentional UV elements glowing?
Counterfeit cards often use brightened paper or PVC that glows blue under UV.

hints.quote: The card substrate shall be manufactured from materials that do not fluoresce under UV illumination.

hints: Does the portrait image NOT fluoresce or glow under UV light?
Photo substitution using certain materials may cause the portrait to glow under UV.

hints.quote: The portrait image shall be applied using materials that do not fluoresce under ultraviolet illumination.

hints: Under UV light, is there a repeating pattern (e.g., 'CALIFORNIA' text or state outline) visible that is invisible under white light?

hints.source: California DMV Driver License & ID Card Design Specification (Gen 3)
hints.quote: A UV-visible background pattern consisting of repeating state text or outline shall be present.

sections.title: Card Back & PDF417 Barcode
hints: Is a PDF417 barcode present on the back of the card?

hints.quote: All AAMVA-compliant driver's licenses shall include a PDF417 two-dimensional barcode on the back of the card.

hints: Does the PDF417 barcode scan successfully and return data without error correction failures?
A barcode that fails to scan or returns corrupt data indicates manufacturing defects or tampering.

hints.quote: The PDF417 symbol shall be printed at a quality level sufficient to enable reliable decoding.

hints: Does the barcode header begin with the AAMVA compliance indicator '@\n\x1e\rANSI '?
The file header must start with @ followed by LF, RS, CR, then 'ANSI ' for AAMVA compliance.

hints.quote: The compliance indicator shall be the @ character followed by LF, RS, CR and the characters ANSI followed by a space.

hints: Does the IIN (Issuer Identification Number) in the barcode correspond to California (636014)?

hints.quote: The IIN 636014 is assigned to the state of California.

hints: Is a magnetic stripe absent from the card back (California Gen 3 does not use a mag stripe)?
California dropped the magnetic stripe in earlier generations.

hints.source: California DMV Driver License & ID Card Design Specification (Gen 3)
hints.quote: California Gen 3 driver licenses do not incorporate a magnetic stripe; the PDF417 barcode is the sole machine-readable element.

sections.title: Cross-Field Consistency (Front vs. Barcode)
hints: Does the barcode field DAQ (Customer ID Number / DL number) match the document number printed on the card front?
DAQ must equal the printed DL number exactly.

hints.quote: DAQ contains the unique number assigned to the customer; it shall match the document number displayed on the card.

hints: Does the barcode field DCS (Customer Family Name) match the last name printed on the card front?
Case differences (uppercase vs. mixed) are acceptable; spelling must match.

hints.quote: DCS contains the family name of the cardholder as it appears on the card face.

hints: Does the barcode field DBB (Date of Birth) in MMDDYYYY format match the DOB printed on the card front?

hints.quote: DBB shall contain the cardholder's date of birth in MMDDYYYY format, consistent with the printed date of birth.

hints: Does the barcode field DBA (Document Expiration Date) in MMDDYYYY format match the expiry date printed on the card front?

hints.quote: DBA shall contain the expiration date of the document in MMDDYYYY format.

hints: Does the barcode field DAG (Customer Mailing Street Address) match the address printed on the card front?
Street address in barcode should match front-of-card address.

hints.quote: DAG shall contain the street portion of the mailing address as printed on the card.

hints: Does the barcode field DAI (Customer Mailing City) match the city printed on the card front?

hints.quote: DAI shall contain the city name of the mailing address as printed on the card.

hints: Does the barcode field DAJ (Customer Mailing State) equal 'CA'?

hints.quote: DAJ shall contain the two-letter jurisdiction code; for California this is CA.

hints: Does the barcode field DAK (Customer Mailing ZIP Code) match the ZIP code printed on the card front?
ZIP in barcode is stored as 9-character ZIP+4 padded with trailing spaces or zeros; compare first 5 digits.

hints.quote: DAK shall contain the postal code of the mailing address; for U.S. addresses this is a 9-character ZIP+4.

hints: Does the barcode field DBC (Customer Sex) match the sex/gender designator printed on the card front?

hints.quote: DBC codes: 1 for male, 2 for female, 9 for not specified.

hints: Does the barcode field DAY (Customer Eye Color) match the eye color abbreviation printed on the card front?

hints.quote: DAY shall contain the AAMVA eye color code consistent with the printed eye color on the card face.

sections.title: REAL ID Compliance Markers
hints: Is 'FEDERAL LIMITS APPLY' absent from this card? (Its presence would indicate a non-REAL-ID card.)
California prints 'FEDERAL LIMITS APPLY' on non-REAL-ID cards. Absence confirms REAL ID compliance.

hints.source: REAL ID Act of 2005 / 6 CFR Part 37
hints.quote: Cards not meeting REAL ID requirements shall clearly state on the face that the card is not acceptable for federal purposes.

hints: Has the applicant provided proof of identity, SSN, and California residency during issuance (document-level check via DMV records, not verifiable on card face alone)?
REAL ID requires collection of these documents; the card does not encode proof but should be consistent with verified demographics.

hints.source: REAL ID Act of 2005 / 6 CFR Part 37
hints.quote: An applicant for a REAL ID-compliant driver's license shall provide documentary evidence of identity, Social Security Account Number, and principal residence.

hints: Does the card contain the issuing state's full name and the state abbreviation 'CA' in the machine-readable zone or barcode?

hints.source: REAL ID Act of 2005 / 6 CFR Part 37
hints.quote: REAL ID driver's licenses shall contain the full legal name of the holder and the State that issued the card.

sections.title: Address & Jurisdictional Data
hints: Is the address on the card front a California address (not another state)?
California DLs are only issued to California residents.

hints.source: REAL ID Act of 2005 / 6 CFR Part 37
hints.quote: Each applicant for a REAL ID card shall present documentation showing the applicant's principal address in the issuing state.

hints: Is the ZIP code on the card front a valid California ZIP code (beginning with 900–961)?

hints.source: California DMV Driver License & ID Card Design Specification (Gen 3)
hints.quote: The ZIP code printed on the card shall correspond to a valid California address.

hints: Is the state field on the card front printed as 'CA'?

hints.quote: The two-character USPS abbreviation for the issuing state shall appear in the address block.

sections.title: Endorsements, Restrictions & Class
hints: Is the license class printed on the card and does it correspond to a valid California DL class (C, A, B, or M)?
Class C is the standard passenger vehicle license. A and B are commercial. M is motorcycle.

hints.quote: Driver's license classes in California are Class A, Class B, Class C, and Class M.

hints: Are any endorsements (e.g., H, N, P, S, T, X) and restrictions (e.g., A, B, C, L) printed on the card consistent with the license class?
Motorcycle (M) endorsement should not appear on a Class A or B CDL card without separate notation.

hints.source: California Vehicle Code §12804.9 and 49 CFR Part 383
hints.quote: Endorsements and restrictions shall be printed on the license and shall correspond to the license class and holder's qualifications.

hints: If the cardholder is under 21, is the card printed in a vertical (portrait) orientation rather than horizontal (landscape)?
California issues vertical-format DLs to those under 21 as a visual age indicator.

hints.source: California Vehicle Code §12814.5 and California DMV Design Spec Gen 3
hints.quote: A driver's license issued to a person under 21 years of age shall be designed so that it is readily distinguishable from licenses issued to persons 21 years of age or older; California uses vertical orientation.
